characters.name: Achmed Abjeer
characters.description: First Appearance: The Road to Dendron Occupation: The Prince of Dendron. Hungry for the throne, he concocted a plan to kill the Princess and Sulton, which for unknown reasons involved kidnapping Ajax. (When he tried to explain why, a camel chewing hay steps in front of the camera and drowns him out.) This proved to be his undoing however, as Ajax and Princess Fallopia were near identical, Ajax switched places with her and she and her father survived. He has his very own tent of horror, but it has a problem with gnats. Also, he never should have installed that water slide.

characters.name: Agent Dennehy
characters.description:
Agent Hank DennehyGeneralGenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationIRS AgentRelationshipsLove InterestsBernice Hufnagel(momentarily)EnemiesEric DuckmanBehind the ScenesVoiced byJohn BynerFirst Appearance"Not So Easy Riders"Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable" (cameo) Agent Hank Dennehyis a ruthless IRS Agent who once went afterDuckmanfor his back taxes. He is a recurring character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, however he only has a primary role in theseason 1episode "Not So Easy Riders". When first introduced, Dennehy's ruthlessness is demonstrated through his heartless refusal to be lenient with his own father-in-law merely over a smudged receipt, despite him having a very good and surprisingly true reason for it. He also harshly addresses Duckman, who was initially unaware of his legal predicament. While Duckman is on the run from the IRS, Dennehy stays at the Duckman household, waiting for the inevitable moment that Duckman calls home.
During this time, Dennehy develops a relationship withBernice. At the house, he also plays withAjax,Charles and Mambo. He eventually takes Bernice and the kids to Las Vegas, where he happens to run into Duckman and nearly arrests him; the day is saved by Cornfed, however, who had just finished winning all of the money that he and Duckman owed. Without Duckman to pursue, Dennehy instantly loses interest in Bernice and her nephews. Dennehy only serves as a background character in any of the other episodes that he appears in. Later on in the episode "Where No Duckman Has Gone Before", he is part of Captain Duckman's crew.

characters.name: Agnes Delrooney
characters.description: Agnes DelrooneyGeneralGenderFemaleSpeciesDuckOccupationCriminalResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CA (formerly)Behind the ScenesVoiced byBrian Doyle-MurrayFirst Appearance"Aged Heat"Last Appearance"You've Come a Wrong Way, Baby"Agnes Delrooneyis a recurring character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. To evade the law, she tookGrandma-ma's place within the household after getting Grandma-ma thrown in jail. Agnes Delrooney was a notorious organized crime matriarch. In "Aged Heat", after robbing a jewelry store at the mall, she spots Grandma-ma in the car parked outside. Realizing she looks exactly like her, Agnes switches places with her and pretends to be her in order to hide out from the police. Agnes Delrooney was a notorious organized crime matriarch. In "Aged Heat", after robbing a jewelry store at the mall, she spots Grandma-ma in the car parked outside. Realizing she looks exactly like her, Agnes switches places with her and pretends to be her in order to hide out from the police. Duckman is the only one she reveals herself to, but the rest of the family doesn't believe him. Eventually, Bernice, Ajax, Charles and Mambo catch her abusing Duckman and realize he was right. She holds the family at gunpoint, sending Duckman to work so as to not raise suspicion. When Duckman returns home, he sees that she has bound and gagged his family, and soon demands to have her way with Duckman. Luckily for him, she is stopped just in time by Cornfed and the FBI. However, Cornfed and Duckman accidentally send Grandma-ma off with authorities after Agnes pulls a switch-out. In "You've Come a Wrong Way, Baby", while the family is being held captive on a tobacco farm, she once again reveals her true identity and flees with Duckman. During their escape, the two actually manage to come to an understanding and even toast whilst receiving awards for bringing down Walt Evergreen, the owner of the tobacco farm. For this deed she is pardoned by the governor and Grandma-ma is released, but she is soon arrested again for having alcohol in a dry zone.

characters.name: Ajax Duckman
characters.description:
This is a featured article, meaning it has been identified as one of the best articles produced by theDuckman Wikicommunity.If you see a way this page can be updated or improved without compromising previous work, please feel free to contribute.Otherwise, don't soil the centerfold! Ajax DuckmanGeneralAliasesAjaxina (byBernicein "Exile in Guyville")Tweedle-What-a-Friggin'-Moron (byMo Dorkin)Age15BirthdayJune 18, 1988GenderMale (possibly identifies as a female, as stated in "My Feral Lady")SpeciesDuckOccupationStudentPoet writer for Watermark Greeting Cards (formerly)ResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsParentsEric Duckman(father)Beatrice Hufnagel-Duckman(mother)RelativesBernice Hufnagel(aunt)Beverly Hufnagel(aunt)Grandma-ma(grandmother)SiblingsCharles and Mambo Duckman(younger brothers)PetsGeckoLove InterestsTammy ChickenFriendsCornfed PigCoolioBehind the ScenesVoiced byDweezil ZappaFirst Appearance"The Case of the Missing Chromosome" (pilot)"I, Duckman"Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable"Ajax Duckmanis one of the main characters in ofDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. He isDuckman's eldest son who takes afterhis mother's heart and his father's low IQ. Ajax speaks with a slight surfer-dude accent, and calls his father "Dod". He is extremely slow mentally and doesn't have any athletic skill, but is a skilled free-verse poet. He has also been depicted as having musical abilities; his singing voice is a powerful soprano, and he can even sing piano music to perfection.He occasionally surprises other characters with a few words of philosophical wisdom or obscure knowledge of his birthday, but this is usually followed by him saying something more inline with his stupidity. Ajax speaks with a slight surfer-dude accent, and calls his father "Dod". He is extremely slow mentally and doesn't have any athletic skill, but is a skilled free-verse poet. He has also been depicted as having musical abilities; his singing voice is a powerful soprano, and he can even sing piano music to perfection.He occasionally surprises other characters with a few words of philosophical wisdom or obscure knowledge of his birthday, but this is usually followed by him saying something more inline with his stupidity. In "Ride the High School", it is revealed that Ajax's lack of intelligence makes it so that he is the only member of the family to whom Duckman can truly relate. Aside from his low IQ, his most notable trait is his emotional frailty. Ajax often feels rejected by his family and peers, which can result in overwhelming feelings of sadness, and desperation to be accepted. Ajax got his name from a trucking company Cornfed was driving for when he and Duckman first met (however, due to continuity errors, this was technically the second time they'd met). 
Ajax is a hugeMerv Griffinfan, and constantly attempts to call him by phone so they can "always have a working line between [them]".

He was a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, appearing only in theseason 4episode "Crime, Punishment, War, Peace, and the Idiot".He was portrayed byAjax DuckmanthroughoutGrandma-ma's flashback. Not much is known about his character, however he seemed to be a town crier and/or mail carrier of some sort. He announced to the town that Russia was at war, and was also the one who deliveredTrigorinhis draft letter. characters.name: Amanda and Alexis
characters.description: Amanda and AlexisGeneralAliasesSweetmeats (by theMcGarrigle Twins)GenderFemalesSpeciesDucksOccupationStudentsResidenceLos AngelesRelationshipsEnemiesMcGarrigle TwinsBehind the ScenesVoiced byMary Ellen DunbarandJulie MaddalenaOnly Appearance"The Girls of Route Canal"Amanda and Alexisare one-time characters inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. They are students at Mid City Junior High, the same school thatCharles and Mamboattend. Amanda and Alexis were the girlsCharles and Mambohad crushes on in "The Girls of Route Canal". They were dating theMcGarrigle Twins, although it would seem not by choice. The McGarrigles would often drag them by their arms, and refer to them not by name, but by unsavory nicknames. Amanda and Alexis were the girlsCharles and Mambohad crushes on in "The Girls of Route Canal". They were dating theMcGarrigle Twins, although it would seem not by choice. The McGarrigles would often drag them by their arms, and refer to them not by name, but by unsavory nicknames. They were quite obviously more interested in Charles and Mambo, and were bullied nearly just as bad by their boyfriends as the twins were. By the end of the episode, Charles and Mambo had the McGarrigles deported to Albania, so the four of them could get time together at last.

characters.name: America
characters.description: AmericaGeneralGenderFemaleSpeciesHumanOccupationSupermodelResidenceMunicipal Dump and Landfill, Los Angeles, CA (formerly)RelationshipsPartnersWilbur Nelson(ex-boyfriend)Cinque(ex-boyfriend)Saul Monella(ex-boyfriend)Stone(ex-fiancé)Behind the ScenesVoiced byHeather LocklearOnly Appearance"America the Beautiful"Americawas a beloved supermodel who once isolated herself from the world due to her previous relationships and mistakes.WithDuckman's help, however, she realizes that she can learn from her past and lead a brighter future. She is a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, appearing only in theseason 2episode "America the Beautiful". In "America the Beautiful", she was severely depressed and had planned on committing suicide.Duckmaneventually found her living in the city dump, feeling used up and uncared for after her previous four relationships. In "America the Beautiful", she was severely depressed and had planned on committing suicide.Duckmaneventually found her living in the city dump, feeling used up and uncared for after her previous four relationships. Thefirst of whichwas a buttoned-down, old-fashioned values misogynist who didn't believe women should question his authority. Thesecondwas a hippie college student who said that America was yearning to be free when they met. He got her into experimenting with drugs, which apparently resulted in such new experiences as trying to tie her shoes after being up for a week and staring at her hand all day. She then dateda disco dancerwho was callous and vain, caring only about having a good time. Herlast boyfriendwas a ruthless Wall Street executive who would put anybody down in order to make a profit. When Duckman finally finds America, she is ready to give up on her life.He tells her that there are times where he, too, wants to give up, but "if I do, how can I expect anything to get better? What kind of a world would I be leaving for my kids? Don't give up, America. You've gotta keep going, for your kids."Taking these words into consideration, and seeing how much the young fans in her fan club idolize her, she is given the strength to go on.

When Duckman finally finds America, she is ready to give up on her life.He tells her that there are times where he, too, wants to give up, but "if I do, how can I expect anything to get better? What kind of a world would I be leaving for my kids? Don't give up, America. You've gotta keep going, for your kids."Taking these words into consideration, and seeing how much the young fans in her fan club idolize her, she is given the strength to go on.

They dated for a short period of time, and she is nowDuckman's ex-girlfriend. She is a recurring character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. She is introduced in "About Face", where Mambo accidentally swallows and chokes on a model of the Titanic. Duckman rushes to the phone, and dials 911, where one of the operators picks up. Angela, of course, was that operator. She is introduced in "About Face", where Mambo accidentally swallows and chokes on a model of the Titanic. Duckman rushes to the phone, and dials 911, where one of the operators picks up. Angela, of course, was that operator. After the Titanic sunk out of Mambo's throat, Duckman became obsessed with her and her angelic voice. Since the dispatchers refuse to give Duckman her name, he stages multiple 911 emergencies withFluffy and Uranustrying to get her to pick up again. He doesn't find her, but she eventually calls back to check how Mambo was doing, as he was the first life she helped save. Duckman asks her out and she agrees. When they meet it turns out that she is hideously ugly, so much so that even Fluffy and Uranus reacted in terror when they saw her in a photo. Duckman is still in love with her though, describing her as "smart, sensitive, cheerful, thought provoking and loaded with empathy and insight." When they go to a restaurant withCornfedandTrudyone night, Duckman ends up punching a waiter after the waiter makes fun of her face. This prompts Angela to get a makeover, eventually becoming America's most famous supermodel. Duckman decides that she is too good for him now, and that he would only be holding her back, so he breaks up with her. Angela later appears in "Color of Naught". She was a spokeswoman forBeautex, not realizing it was another one ofKing Chicken's plans to get back at Duckman. It's revealed that she had waited for Duckman to show up in that restaurant for three weeks. She kept ordering iced tea, which slowly made one of her kidneys shut down. After the surgery to get that fixed, she vowed never to rely on others. She tries to tell Duckman that she's happier staying closed off, but she hardly gets through saying it without breaking down. Duckman tells her why he didn't show up, and after a moment of silence, Angela approaches him again, and sweeps him into a kiss. At the end of the episode, Duckman asks Angela for her hand in marriage. She rejects, however. She reflects on the Beautex slogan "Because in this world you come first", and how she was the one who thought of that slogan. characters.description: Art DeSalvoGeneralAliasesDr. Bones McSalvo(Star Trekcounterpart)GenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationPublicistBusinessmanFreelance PR consultantResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsRelativesScott DeSalvo(likely his son)Behind the ScenesVoiced byGilbert GottfriedFirst Appearance"Papa Oom M.O.W. M.O.W."Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable" (cameo)Art DeSalvois a dishonest scam artist that often takes advantage ofDuckmanand his family. He is a recurring character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. Cornfeddescribes him as a "fast-talking, deeply cynical and completely amoral manipulator." Cornfeddescribes him as a "fast-talking, deeply cynical and completely amoral manipulator." In "Papa Oom M.O.W. M.O.W.", he books Duckman on a talk show to discuss his heroism, but ends up revealing that he pinched the asses of two women, igniting an angry mob and turning him into America's number one deviant. In "The Longest Weekend", it's shown that Art has moved into Duckman's neighborhood, N Phlegm Ave, and joins in the block association that's driven to destroy Dutch Elm St on account of the latter street's lobbying. He was also said to be an expert on organizational structures. In "How to Suck in Business Without Really Trying", he cons Duckman into signing over his own name and persona for $1,000. He also notes that he was on his way to see his podiatrist because of his spring corn-scrape. In "Four Weddings Inconceivable", it is said that he has a child or teenage relative named Scott DeSalvo, who has a strange interest in Mambo. It is unstated how the two are related, but since Scott DeSalvo was placed at "the kiddy table with all the neighbor kids", it can be inferred that he is Art DeSalvo's son. It is unknown how they behave toward each other as Scott is an unseen character, and Art never mentions Scott himself.

characters.name: Aunt Jane Cornfed
characters.description: Jane CornfedGeneralAliasesAunt JaneAunt something-or-other (byDuckman)GenderFemaleSpeciesPigOccupationRanch ownerResidenceJane's Ranch, LouisianaRelationshipsRelativesCornfed Pig(nephew)SiblingsJohn Cornfed(brother or brother-in-law)Cornfed's Mother(sister or sister-in-law)Behind the ScenesVoiced byEstelle GettyOnly Appearance"Westward, No!"Aunt Jane CornfedisCornfed's aunt who owns a catfish farm in Louisiana. She is a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, appearing only in theseason 4episode “Westward, No!”. She is seen to be sweet and mild-mannered, but can easily get tough when she needs to be. She is seen to be sweet and mild-mannered, but can easily get tough when she needs to be. She's been shown to be good at raising animals and taking care of plants. When she first appears, she's finishing up her tomato plants before she turns around and sees her nephew Cornfed at her front step. When Cornfed takesBeverlyandDuckmanto visit her, they find that her farm is experiencing financial difficulties; she can barely make her loan payments these days. Her farmhands are fixing to drive the herd to Texas, and it's her last chance to save the ranch where her father and grandfather both lived and died. Big Jack McBastardfinds that Jane had to fire the farmhands on account of their assault on Duckman. Due to this, Cornfed, Beverly, and—of course—Duckman had to drive the catfish to Texas themselves. Otherwise, Jane would lose everything.

characters.name: Baron von Dillweed
characters.description: Baron von DillweedGeneralAliasesDilly (by Bernice)GenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationNetwork headResidenceLos AngelesBehind the ScenesVoiced byDavid RascheOnly AppearanceMarried AliveBaron von Dillweedis a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. He is a giga-billionaire entrepreneur who owns a home shopping network, named BVD, where America shops in it's underwear. Baron von Dillweed was a millionaire who owned the home shopping network, BVD, which had the slogan "Stop the Inanity". He metBerniceat a resort in Mexico and used her desperation to manipulate her into marrying him, so that he could adopt her nephews, as he had been told by his accountant that having a wife and children would make him more relatable to his key demographic. Baron von Dillweed was a millionaire who owned the home shopping network, BVD, which had the slogan "Stop the Inanity". He metBerniceat a resort in Mexico and used her desperation to manipulate her into marrying him, so that he could adopt her nephews, as he had been told by his accountant that having a wife and children would make him more relatable to his key demographic. He planned to broadcast this marriage live on his network, and then take her and the children to live in Switzerland. He seduced the boys with expensive gifts thatDuckmancould never afford, while also exploiting a loophole inBeatrice's will. He offered Bernice the job of spokeswoman for his new product, a glorified rubber band referred to as the "Rubbercizer". While putting forward his detective skills, Duckman discovered that Dillweed had accidentally bought a glass eye company and had thousands of useless glass eyes in a warehouse which he planned on selling to people once his shoddy Rubbercizer product caused it's users to lose an eye. Duckman confronts him with this information in the middle of the wedding, which he confesses to. An angry Bernice puts his eye out with one of the rubber bands before he is able to make his escape.

characters.name: Barry Brittle
characters.description: Barry BrittleGeneralGenderMaleSpeciesHumanOccupationCreative Vice President at Watermark Greeting CardsRelationshipsEnemiesEric DuckmanBehind the ScenesVoiced byEd Begley Jr.First Appearance"Research and Destroy"Last Appearance"They Craved Duckman's Brain!" (cameo) Barry Brittleis a slimy businessman who was once a creative V.P. at a greeting card company. He is a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, only having a primary role in theseason 2episode "Research and Destroy". Barry Brittle heard Ajax's poetry and offered him a job at a greeting card company. However, he only cared about the bottom line and had no artistic integrity, so he wouldn't allow Ajax to write in his own way.He relied entirely on graphs created by data from focus groups in his suggestions for Ajax's poems. When Ajax tells him he changed his mind about the job, Barry threatens him, telling him his family will be bankrupt if he leaves. Eventually, Duckman accidentally destroys his focus group data collecting supercomputer, resulting in Ajax's firing, and Barry vowing never to try anything new or creative ever again. When first speaking with Duckman, Barry admits that he "used the gay label as an accessory" to earn himself affirmative action promotions.
characters.personality_section: Barry Brittle heard Ajax's poetry and offered him a job at a greeting card company. characters.name: Beatrice Hufnagel-Duckman
characters.description: This article contains information that spoils the series finale. Read at your own risk.Beatrice Hufnagel-DuckmanCurrentFarmerGeneralStatusDeceasedGenderFemaleSpeciesDuckResidenceRoute Canal (formerly)N Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsParentsGrandma-ma(mother)Trigorin Hufnagel(biological father;deceased prior to birth)Unnamed step-fatherSiblingsBernice Hufnagel(sister)Beverly Hufnagel(sister)SpousesRichard(ex-husband)Eric Duckman(husband)ChildrenAjax Duckman(eldest son)Charles and Mambo Duckman(twin sons)Behind the ScenesVoiced byNancy TravisFirst Appearance"The Case of the Missing Chromosome" (with Bernice's design and personality)"I, Duckman" (as herself)Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable" Beatrice Eleanor Hufnagel-DuckmanwasDuckman's very beautiful wife and the mother ofAjax,Charles and Mambo. She is a recurring character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, although she only appears throughout the series in flashbacks, dreams, and photos. She grew up in North Dakota, somewhere she describes as "a place with a way of life that was so different... even primitive". Duckman met her in a farming county in Iowa while working for a magazine that sent him to take pictures of covered bridges. They spent a lot of time together and quickly fell in love. Beatrice seemed to share a bit of Duckman's sexual perversion, and even states that Duckman kind of reminds her of her papa. However, she turned out to be married to a chicken namedRichard, whom she states she loves but is notinlove with. Richard had a heart attack inadvertently caused by Duckman, who told him the truth of their affair in overbearing detail. Soon afterwards, she found that she was pregnant with Duckman's first son, Ajax. The two were married for roughly fifteen years, until she was killed in an accident caused by Duckman himself (he had flicked his cigarette out into the street during a Thanksgiving parade and it hit a balloon which deflated and caused a chain reaction that knocked her down a manhole). Duckman idealizes his love for her. Despite his puerile tendencies, he never cheated on her and felt extremely guilty about pursuing other relationships even a year after she had died. Not much is known about her extensive life with her family, as most of what we know about her comes from the memories of her husband, children and sister. In all of these memories she is remembered as being wonderful to those around her. In "Four Weddings Inconceivable", it is revealed thatshe'd been alive this whole time. Cornfed apparently knew about this, but never told Duckman for unknown reasons.

(byDuckman)Lard-bottomed Freak (byCornfedin "Role With It")GenderFemaleSpeciesDuckOccupationSinger (formerly)CongresswomanResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsParentsGrandma-ma(mother)Trigorin Hufnagel(biological father;deceased prior to birth)Unnamed step-fatherRelativesAjax Duckman(nephew)Charles and Mambo Duckman(nephews)SiblingsBeatrice Hufnagel-Duckman(sister)Beverly Hufnagel(sister)PetsGeckoLove InterestsKing ChickenPast Love Interests:Baron Von Dillweed(in "Married Alive")New Duckman(inthe game)FriendsCornfedEnemiesEric DuckmanHoney ChickenBehind the ScenesVoiced byAndrea Martin(pilot)Nancy TravisFirst Appearance"The Case of the Missing Chromosome" (design only)"I, Duckman" (as Bernice)Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable"Bernice Florence Hufnagel, often referred to asAunt Bernice, is one of the main characters ofDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. She isDuckman's buxom and opinionated sister-in-law who takes any opportunity to undercut his authority. Past Love Interests: Bernice was described byDr. Susan Foxas "a domineering mother figure who delights in cruelly emasculating the men in her life." She is the identical triplet ofBeatriceandBeverly. She is a fanatic fitness buff and hates Duckman with a passion, often mocking him alongside her signature cackling laugh. Though she loathes and reviles their father, Bernice is very loving to her nephews and is considered to be their primary caretaker. Despite this, Duckman views her as an abrasive, domineering, self-righteous shrew who constantly tries to control his life. Despite being physically identical to his wife, Duckman harbors no physical attraction to her. In fact, when she ordered him to lotion her back, he put a glove on his hand and expressed disgust. However, they once engaged in sex while intoxicated. She eventually was appointed as a congresswoman, because her family values came to popular light by destroying her singing career for her nephews. She was concerned about leaving them with Duckman, until Beverly found her way to the family. So she left them in Beverly's care, still visiting home occasionally to catch up.

characters.description: BettyRed hairBlonde hairGeneralAliasesOld pal (byDuckman)GenderFemaleSpeciesHumanRelationshipsFriendsDuckmanBehind the ScenesVoiced bySheena EastonOnly Appearance"Aged Heat 2: Women in Heat" Bettyis a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, appearing only in theseason 4episode, "Aged Heat 2: Women in Heat". Betty was Duckman's cellmate at the Federal Women's Penitentiary he was accidentally sent to. Betty was Duckman's cellmate at the Federal Women's Penitentiary he was accidentally sent to. She was onceWandaand theWarden's top dancer/prostitute, and enjoyed all the money and popularity that went with it, before being quickly replaced. She tried to warn Duckman that he was letting himself fall into the same trap, but he believed there could never be anyone better than him. She did not think Duckman was anything special before he came to prison, but she obviously valued their friendship (something Duckman did not return, as he could not even remember her name). Along with the rest of the inmates, Betty was freed from the prison when Cornfed revealed the exploitations that had been going on.

characters.name: Beverly Hufnagel
characters.description: Beverly HufnagelGeneralFull NameBeverly Glenn HufnagelAliasesBevBevertriceGenderFemaleSpeciesDuckOccupationHomemakerResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsParentsGrandma-ma(mother)Trigorin Hufnagel(biological father;deceased prior to birth)RelativesAjax Duckman(eldest nephew)Charles and Mambo Duckman(nephews)SiblingsBernice Hufnagel(sister)Beatrice Hufnagel(sister)PartnersCornfed PigFriendsCornfed PigBehind the ScenesVoiced byNancy TravisFirst Appearance"Bev Takes a Holiday"Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable"Beverly Glenn Hufnagelis introduced withinseason 4as one of the main characters ofDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man.She is the long-lost triplet sister ofBerniceandBeatrice. She takes Bernice's place in the household when Bernice was elected to Congress. It was revealed in thetwelfth episode of the fourth seasonthatBerniceandBeatricewere separated from their triplet sister at birth. When Duckman mistook her for his deceased wife, Beatrice, he chased after her, and ran into a truck. To help him pull through, Beverly made him continue to believe that she really was Beatrice, giving him the will to keep alive. It was revealed in thetwelfth episode of the fourth seasonthatBerniceandBeatricewere separated from their triplet sister at birth. When Duckman mistook her for his deceased wife, Beatrice, he chased after her, and ran into a truck. To help him pull through, Beverly made him continue to believe that she really was Beatrice, giving him the will to keep alive. Prior to moving into the Duckman household, Beverly mentioned a failed marriage of hers, on top of a series of bad relationships. It left her searching for a bond to anyone. She hired a local detective to seek out her blood relatives, where she learned that she's a triplet. She was scared to meet her biological relatives because she felt she would be practically walking into a stranger's life. She is much more understanding to Duckman than Bernice is, and sees the good he has to offer, though she never ceases to be disgusted or mildly offended by his overall behavior. Her understanding of Duckman may come from her own low-key perversity, as she mentions that she has a subscription toPlaygirl, which she cites as the reason why she's fine with Duckman'sPenthouseenthusiasm. Duckman often attempted to woo her, however Beverly took more of a romantic interest inCornfed, and the feeling was mutual. Other than just having a different hairstyle comparing to Bernice's, Bev is distinguished by her blue eyes. Bev is often flirted with, which seems to annoy her quite a bit. Even the detective that tracked down her blood relatives offered an option to reduce her pay by partaking in sexual intercourse with him, to which she simply takes the file and bluntly walks off. She's a kind soul overall, but she's well aware of when to draw the line, even with Duckman.

characters.name: Bit Characters
characters.description: This is a list of characters who do not have any major bearing on the plot, reoccur with different personalities to fit the story, or background props who are re-used enough to be recognizable. Duckman's neighbours, excluding Dr. Ben Stein, appear often but with interchangeable personalities and usually only as a group, or to fill backgrounds. Strippers and prostitutes hired by Duckman or women (and occasional men) Duckman hits on fall under this category. They may not necessarily be a sex worker, bimbo or slut, but they are identified by their similar character designs. Big eyes, long legs and an hour-glass figure, usually taken to the point of absurdity. They often wear the same type of choker necklace with a gold charm hanging off it. Some characters like this are more relevant to the story, likeSherryorFantine and Cosette. These are the more incidental characters.

characters.name: Bob Heiny
characters.description: Bob HeinyGeneralPejorative AliasesWyatt (byDuckman)Hopalong (byDuckman)Fry boy (byDuckman)GenderMaleOccupationGeneral Manager of the De Ree's Waffles and Roast Beef on a Stick CorporationBehind the ScenesVoiced byMichael McKeanOnly Appearance"A Civil War"Bob Heinywas the general manager of the De Ree Waffles and Roast Beef on a Stick Corporation. He is a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, only appearing in theseason 1episode "A Civil War". Upon meetingDuckmanandCornfedat the De Ree Waffles and Roast Beef on a Stick factory, Bob Heiny was very welcoming and greeted them with Southern hospitality. He was initially unsure of why Duckman and Cornfed were there, but he still gave them a short tour of the factory as he led them to the current owner (Mrs. Val De Ree)'s office for their investigation. He also openly admitted that the roast beef in their products was partially made up of rat meat as a way to combat their rat infestation. Upon meetingDuckmanandCornfedat the De Ree Waffles and Roast Beef on a Stick factory, Bob Heiny was very welcoming and greeted them with Southern hospitality. He was initially unsure of why Duckman and Cornfed were there, but he still gave them a short tour of the factory as he led them to the current owner (Mrs. Val De Ree)'s office for their investigation. He also openly admitted that the roast beef in their products was partially made up of rat meat as a way to combat their rat infestation. After Duckman had fired Cornfed from being his detective partner, Cornfed applied for any possible opening at the De Ree factory. Bob was impressed with Cornfed's extensive work history, so he gave him a job in the mailroom. Bob then gave him numerous promotions on his first workday for his outstanding performance. Ultimately, Cornfed only wanted a job at the factory to continue working on the case even without Duckman. Despite Bob's constant sincerity and warmth, Cornfed had eventually discovered that Bob was the one who murderedStanley De Ree. Bob knew thatVal De Reewould leave for Rio after collecting her inheritance from her husband's death, so he disguised himself as her and took over the business. Bob confessed that he did all this because he wanted to be Val; he wanted to "set free the girlish passion that was dying for a voice".

characters.name: Brad Gililand
characters.description: Brad GililandCurrentHomelessHigh SchoolGeneralGenderMaleSpeciesDuckOccupationFashion designerRelationshipsParentsUnnamed parentsPetsSparkle(species unknown)FriendsEric DuckmanCornfed PigBehind the ScenesVoiced byPeter ScolariFirst Appearance"From Brad to Worse"Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable" (cameo) Brad GililandisDuckmanandCornfed's friend from high school. He had idolized Duckman and saw him as a role model. He is a one-time character inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man, appearing only in theseason 4episode "From Brad to Worse". Years later, Duckman comes across Brad and discovers he is now homeless. Duckman presumes this is because of how he was expelled after taking credit for a dangerous and elaborate prank that a young Duckman pulled onPrincipal Dinkler. After the prank, Brad and his family were forced to move several times, because everywhere they went, somebody would learn about the "Dinkler Incident". Eventually, he ran away from his parents in order to give them a chance to live, and ended up homeless from then on. Duckman tries to make it up to Brad by taking him into his home, getting him cleaned up and finding him a job and housing. Cornfed, meanwhile, decides to investigate the incident, to find out who actually pulled the fateful prank. After the two are thrown in prison for credit fraud thanks to Duckman, Brad reveals to Social Services that during all these years, he'd been sitting on a brilliant invention. A suit that changes color to indicate ones mood, entitled: the "Mood Suit". Soon after, Duckman and Brad are released of charges, and Brad's Mood Suit is bought by a rich man in the rag trade, and Brad is given a job by him. Brad, Duckman and Cornfed are later tranquilized and kidnapped by Principal Dinkler, where they all confess and discover that all three of them had a hand in the prank that started the whole thing. As punishment, Principal Dinkler makes the three sit in his office taped to chairs for a few hours.

characters.name: Charles and Mambo Duckman
characters.description: This is a featured article, meaning it has been identified as one of the best articles produced by theDuckman Wikicommunity.If you see a way this page can be updated or improved without compromising previous work, please feel free to contribute.Otherwise, don't soil the centerfold! Charles and Mambo DuckmanGeneralAliasesBoth: Deep Throat (inDuckman #5)Charles: Chad, ChazMisnamings of Mambo: La Bamba, Mandingo, Simba, Gumbel, Charles' Brother, Sambo, Simba, Merengue, McKimba, MonoMisnamings of Charles: ChanceMisnamings of Both: Uma, Linda LovelacePejorative AliasesDouble Bill (byAjaxin "American Dicks")Age10GenderMaleSpeciesDuckOccupationStudentsResidenceN Phlegm Ave, Los Angeles, CARelationshipsParentsEric Duckman(father)Beatrice Hufnagel-Duckman(mother)RelativesBernice Hufnagel(aunt)Beverly Hufnagel(aunt)Grandma-ma(grandmother)SiblingsAjax Duckman(older brother)PetsGeckoLove InterestsAmanda and AlexisEnemiesMcGarrigle TwinsBehind the ScenesVoiced byCharles:Dana Hill(1994-1996),Pat Musick(1997)Mambo:Elizabeth DailyFirst Appearance"I, Duckman"Last Appearance"Four Weddings Inconceivable"Charles and Mambo Duckmanare two of the main characters inDuckman: Private Dick/Family Man. They areDuckman's second-born children who take after their father's snarky personality, but definitely not his IQ. Charles and Mambo are child geniuses whose heads share a body. They view Duckman as a subpar and inadequate father (both very true assessments), but love him anyhow. Charles and Mambo are child geniuses whose heads share a body. They view Duckman as a subpar and inadequate father (both very true assessments), but love him anyhow. Duckman can rarely seem to remember Mambo's name and has called him everything from "La Bamba," "Mandingo," and "Simba," to simply "Charles' brother." When arguing, they have been known to head-butt one another, or strangle each other. In later episodes, occasionally one will try to commandeer their shared body; interestingly Charles is able to succeed in doing so multiple times, but Mambo only can on rare occasion. Like Ajax, Charles and Mambo don't have any friends, and are seen to be mocked at school for their nerdy traits. In "The Girls of Route Canal", Charles and Mambo try to speak totheir crush, who was another double-headed oddity. They were pushed aside by theMcGarrigle Twins, who seemed to be dating them by force. Despite their distaste for Duckman's personality, the two actually share several traits with him, such as: their talent for creatively insulting people, their short temper, Mambo's smoking, lack of popularity and friends, physical weakness, large egos, cantankerous attitudes, and reluctances to admit to being wrong.

characters.personality_section: Cornfed is Duckman's Joe Friday-esque business partner and best friend; loosely based on the character "Greggery Peccary" as portrayed by Frank Zappa in a handful of songs. Affable, studious, as well as morally and sexually scrupulous, Cornfed is an amazingly talented pig and has specialist knowledge from practically every walk of life. He knows dozens of martial arts and claims to know "200 ways to kill a man".
